pages.title: Vampire Clothing
pages.text: Vampires have a thing about looking dapper, and they have a few clothing options available to them that is made from a special material that gives some bonuses to their normal skills. Vampire clothing is made from $(l:resources/woven_cruor)Woven Cruor$(/l). Vampires often accompany their special clothing with a $(l:artifacts/weapons/cane_sword)Cane Sword$(/l).

pages.title: Light & Medium Armors
pages.text: Vampire clothing falls into two categories, either $(l:artifacts/clothing/vampire_clothing#light)Light Armor$() or $(l:artifacts/clothing/vampire_clothing#medium)Medium Armor$(). Medium armor gives more protection, but offers less bonuses than a full set of light armor. A Vampire may wear normal heavy armor but then they will have no bonuses in exchange for better armor protection.

pages.title: Light Armor
pages.description: Armor values like $(item)Leather$(), durability of $(item)Iron$(), repaired with $(item)Leather$(). Chest and Hat can be dyed.

pages.title: Special Properties
pages.text: This set has the following bonuses depending on the number of pieces worn:$(p)$(8)> $()$(bold)2 Pieces$(): Drink Speed Boost$(br)$(8)> $()$(bold)3 Pieces$(): Basic Fire Resistance & Mesmerize Duration Boost$(br)$(8)> $()$(bold)4 Pieces$(): Enhanced Fire Resistance.

pages.title: Medium Armor
pages.description: Armor values like $(item)Chain$(), durability of $(item)Iron$(), repaired with $(item)Leather$(). Chest can be dyed.

pages.title: Special Properties
pages.text: This set has the following bonuses depending on the number of pieces worn:$(p)$(8)> $()$(bold)2 Pieces$(): Basic Flame Protection.
